garden.title: Digital Garden
garden.content:
This page is inspired by Karlicoss’s exobrain
I want to make a better record of who I am, and the content I am interacting
with. Eventually want to consolidate my bookmarks to make something like
Nikiv.dev’s Wiki
I have thousands of bookmarks between Pocket (rip), Raindrop, and GitHub
stars.
I’m going to use this page as a hub of all of the links and backlog that I have
to read, and as I make my way through them I’ll create pages with notes

Want to learn
Different tech and concepts I find interesting and cool links I want to look at more.
Game Dev
I just think games and their development are the coolest things. I find myself
watching a lot of Extra Credits and Game Maker’s
Toolkit videos.
Below are a few different tutorials and concepts I’ve been meaning to dive into

Box2D tutorial — Struggled to find a good tutorial on this and really understand how to make a good feeling platformer
libtcod — Roguelike dev engine
Game Programming Design Patterns
How to make a .io game
Machinations — Tool to prototype game systems and economies
How to make an RPG
BYTEPATH Tutorial — A really nice
all encompassing tutorial for a Love2D game
Roguelike tutorial
PIDs: Creating Stable Control in Games
World Generation Breakdown
How to Make an RPG

Game AI

Chess Engines
Game AI Pro

Generative AI
While I’ve been working in the Generative AI “industry” for more than a year now
I don’t actually have any classical training with it and have just been picking
up things on the fly. I would like to go back and immerse myself in some kind of
JITT to learn stuff in detail. Some resources I’ve found that I’d like to go
through and potentially review.

ARENA Curriculum
Building LLMs from the Ground Up: A 3-hour Coding Workshop
Building a GPT-like Model from Scratch with Detailed Theory and Code
Implementation
The Annotated Transformer
Let’s build GPT: from scratch, in code, spelled out
Neural Networks: Zero to Hero

Alternate Link


Hugging Face NLP Course

Petal Stack
I’m getting pretty tired of the NodeJS ecosystem and have been reading a lot
about rails and why people really liked it back in the day. After working on a
couple of projects where I had to figure out a ton of stuff like auth myself
along with issues between client side vs server side code the opiniated all
inclusive stack seems really appealing. Elxir + Pheonix + Liveview seems to be
the modern successor in a lot of ways to rails.
Additionally, I’ve been reading a lot of the fly.io blog
posts, and they talk about elixir a lot, which fly being a great platform for
running it. As a fan of fly, and it being the place where I want to host a lot of
projects in the future I want to dive more into it.
Massively concurrrent dataflow programming languages
What even are these, why they stopped being used.

Joule Programming Language
E Programming Language

Emulators and Hardware
Just interested in old hardware and why we need to emulate hardware to play ROMs

Fabien Sanglard — Really cool posts on games and hardware
Rodrigro Copetti Architecture of Consoles
MiSTer FPGA

Compilers, Interpreters, and Parsers
Mainly stemming from a interest in neovim and how text editors work

Crafting Interpreters
How to write a tree-sitter grammar in an afternoon
Let’s Build a Compiler
From Source Code to Machine Code
Writing a compiler in Go
Driving Compilers
Building the fastest Lua interpreter..automatically!
Bril: A Compiler Intermediate Representation for Learning

Operating Systems

Operating Systems: Three Easy Pieces
Hypervisor From Scratch

Browser Engineering

Web Browser Engineering
Understanding WASM

Interface Description Languages

Wikipedia Page
Apache Thrift Project
Imandra.ai: IPL

Protocol Engineering
This comes from looking at a lot of different blockchain projects and noticing
that the base for many of them aren’t a coin but rather a common protocol. I
think my interest in this started back when I discovered IPFS before I found
Filecoin. Then after learning a bit about how a DAO works through sart contracts
I was wondering what else you could do by design domain specific protocols.

Could you maybe design an entire set of laws and decision making using a DAO
protocol

For example could you codify Robert’s Rules of Order into a protocol that
you could then deploy as an instance of a “deliberative assembly”


Also interested in this domain from the space of networking protocols. For the
most part, the different protocols I’ve seen are fundamentally based on TCP and UDP.
So how can you go about designing your own protocol for specific needs?

Thermodynamic Computing
Since I’ve gotten more active on Twitter over the last year I’ve become a bit
familiarized with Extropic, and keep struggling to
understand what the heck they are talking about.
I can’t tell if it’s a grift, if it’s real, or even really what the value of
what they are talking about is. That said Normal Computing
is apparently working on similar stuff I usually find their posts delightful and
well written. So I want to spend some time diving into that and understanding
what it really is and how it works.

Thermodynamic Computing Beyond Moore’s Law and the von Neumann Bottleneck
Probablistic Computing with P-bits
A First Demonstration of Thermodynamic Matrix Inversion
Thermodynamic Linear Algebra
Thermodynamic AI: intelligence from nature
Extropic Litepaper

Questions I want to explore
Financial Infrastructure
These questions stem from some the work I was doing with
plain-text-accounting and trying to understand
why it’s so hard to get my own financial data, and it’s a bit two-pronged in terms
of what I want to know. First, I want to understand a bit more of creating my
own personal finance stack. This includes the budgeting work I’ve done, but also
stuff such as owning my own bank and investments. Some related platforms I was
looking at.

Column Developer Bank
Composer no-code investment workflows
Alpaca algotrading
Bare Metal Bank Increase

The other idea I was thinking about is what is the actual technology that is at
the backbones of the US or current world financial system. How does the stock
exchange work? I know how a lot of decentralized or centralized exchanges in the
web3 world work, but I don’t fully understand the current stock exchange. If
it’s an exchange why I can trade on it directly? Wanted to frame this as if I
was starting a new country how would I build a financial system.

Electronic Data Interchange
Fednow

Climate
How is technology being leveraged to help with climate change. I’ve seen a lot of different
projects around tokenizing carbon credits, carbon sink technology, advocacy, but want to get more of an
understanding of the landscape, and the current impact it is having.
Some links I’m looking at

Climatebase — list of companies and jobs in the climate space
Crypto Climate Accord — A sorta manifesto about how web3 companies can help with the climate crisis
Climate Collective — Another (rival?) group of web3 organizations trying to help curtail the climate crisis.

index.title: 👋 Welcome to Bor's Blog
index.content:
Welcome to my personal corner of the internet! I’m Dominic Bor, and this is where I share my thoughts, experiments, and learnings.
I have a deep passion for technology and its intersection with intelligence. My primary interests include:

AI & Machine Learning: Exploring the latest in neural networks and generative models.
Data Engineering: Building robust pipelines and architectures for large-scale data processing.
AI Agents: Designing autonomous systems that can reason and act.

Feel free to explore the different sections of this blog:

Garden: My digital garden of interconnected thoughts.
Posts: More structured articles and long-form content.
Projects: A showcase of what I’ve been building.
Notes: Quick captures and reference material.

linktree.title: Personal LinkTree
linktree.content:
My Links

Website (You’re Already Here!)
LinkedIn
GitHub

notes/agentic-workflows.title: Agentic Workflows
notes/agentic-workflows.content: Discussion on common patterns in AI agents: ReAct (Reasoning and Acting), Reflection, and Planning. How to build more autonomous systems.

notes/data-lakehouse.title: Data Lakehouse Architecture
notes/data-lakehouse.content: The evolution from Data Warehouses and Data Lakes to Lakehouses (Delta Lake, Iceberg). ACID transactions on the lake.

notes/dbt-best-practices.title: dbt Best Practices
notes/dbt-best-practices.content: Best practices for organizing dbt models, using macros, and ensuring data quality through tests and documentation.

notes/feature-stores.title: Role of Feature Stores
notes/feature-stores.content: How feature stores like Feast and Tecton simplify the path from data engineering to model training and serving in production.

notes/index.title: Notes
notes/index.content: A collection of quick thoughts, reference materials, and half-baked ideas. This is my digital scratchpad for everything from AI architectures to data engineering snippets.

notes/llm-evaluation.title: LLM Evaluation Frameworks
notes/llm-evaluation.content: Notes on evaluating RAG pipelines using RAGAS and other metrics like faithfulness, answer relevance, and context precision.

notes/multi-agent-systems.title: Multi-Agent Systems
notes/multi-agent-systems.content: Comparing frameworks like AutoGen, CrewAI, and LangGraph for building collaborative AI agent teams.

notes/prompt-engineering.title: Prompt Engineering Techniques
notes/prompt-engineering.content: Exploring Chain-of-Thought, Few-Shot, and Self-Consistency prompting. How to get the most out of LLMs.

notes/pytorch-lightning.title: PyTorch Lightning
notes/pytorch-lightning.content: Simplifying PyTorch model training by abstracting away the boilerplate while maintaining full control. Great for reproducible research.

notes/spark-vs-flink.title: Spark vs Flink
notes/spark-vs-flink.content: Analyzing the trade-offs between Apache Spark and Apache Flink for real-time and batch processing. Spark excels in batch, while Flink is the king of low-latency streaming.

notes/vector-databases.title: Vector Databases: Comparison
notes/vector-databases.content: Comparing popular vector databases like Pinecone, Weaviate, and Milvus for RAG applications. Each has its strengths in scalability and ease of use.

posts/ai-safety-and-alignment.title: AI Safety and Alignment
posts/ai-safety-and-alignment.content: Thinking about the ethical implications of AI and the importance of aligning autonomous systems with human values.

posts/building-a-data-platform-from-scra.title: Building a Data Platform from Scratch
posts/building-a-data-platform-from-scra.content: A deep dive into the architecture of a modern data platform, from ingestion to consumption. We explore the choice of tools and the trade-offs involved in scaling.

posts/fine-tuning-vs-rag.title: Fine-Tuning vs. RAG
posts/fine-tuning-vs-rag.content: When to fine-tune a model and when to use Retrieval-Augmented Generation. Comparing the cost, complexity, and performance of both approaches.

posts/index.title: Posts
posts/index.content: Long-form articles and deep dives into technology, machine learning, and my journey through the world of engineering.

posts/lessons-learned-in-data-engineerin.title: Lessons Learned in Data Engineering
posts/lessons-learned-in-data-engineerin.content: Reflecting on years of building data pipelines. What worked, what didn’t, and why reliability is more important than speed.

posts/modern-data-stack-2025.title: Modern Data Stack 2025
posts/modern-data-stack-2025.content: The state of the data stack in 2025. Which tools are winning, which are losing, and what the next big thing might be.

posts/optimizing-llm-latency.title: Optimizing LLM Latency
posts/optimizing-llm-latency.content: Practical tips for reducing the response time of LLM-based applications, including quantization, caching, and parallel execution.

posts/scalable-ml-deployments.title: Scalable ML Deployments
posts/scalable-ml-deployments.content: Using Kubernetes and specialized tools to deploy machine learning models at scale, ensuring high availability and performance.

posts/the-death-of-the-data-warehouse.title: The Death of the Data Warehouse?
posts/the-death-of-the-data-warehouse.content: Is the traditional data warehouse being replaced by the lakehouse? A look at how technology is converging.

posts/the-future-of-ai-agents.title: The Future of AI Agents
posts/the-future-of-ai-agents.content: Where are AI agents headed? From simple wrappers to autonomous entities that can plan, execute, and learn from their mistakes.

posts/zero-shot-classification-at-scale.title: Zero-Shot Classification at Scale
posts/zero-shot-classification-at-scale.content: Implementing zero-shot text classification using modern LLMs to process millions of documents without labeled data.

projects/Commandline_Calculator.title: Commandline Calculator
projects/Commandline_Calculator.content: A high-performance command-line calculator built with Rust, supporting complex mathematical expressions and unit conversions.

projects/Dukani.title: Dukani
projects/Dukani.content: An e-commerce platform prototype focused on providing local businesses with easy-to-use digital tools for sales and inventory tracking.

projects/Jikoni.title: Jikoni
projects/Jikoni.content: A kitchen management application that helps track inventory, suggest recipes based on available ingredients, and manage shopping lists.

projects/Sirbor.title: Sirbor
projects/Sirbor.content: The codebase for this very blog! Built using Quartz and customized to reflect my journey and interest in technology.

projects/TodoApp.title: TodoApp
projects/TodoApp.content: A minimalistic yet powerful todo application with focus on productivity, offline-first capabilities, and cross-device sync.

projects/iFogSim.title: iFogSim
projects/iFogSim.content: A simulation framework for Fog computing environments, allowing for the modeling of IoT networks and edge-to-cloud data flows.

projects/index.title: Projects
projects/index.content: A showcase of my recent work, ranging from command-line tools to large-scale data systems and AI agent frameworks.
